SpTU 4, 142 [Šumma Izbu 07][via cams/gkab]

oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[BE iz]-⸢bu⸣ x [...]

(o 1') [If an] anomaly [...] ... [...].

o 2'2'

[BE iz]-⸢bu⸣ SAG.[DU] ⸢UGU⸣.DUL.⸢BI⸣ [...] x [...]

(o 2') [If an] anomaly [...] a monkey's head ... [...].

o 3'3'

[BE iz]-bu SAG.DU AZ ( o o ) [...]

(o 3') [If an] anomaly [...] a bear's head [...].

o 4'4'

[BE iz]-bu [SAG].⸢DU⸣ lu-ur-mi [...]

(o 4') [If an] anomaly [...] an ostrich's head [...].

o 5'5'

[BE iz]-bu SAG.DU ( o ) DARA₃ ( o ) [...]

(o 5') [If an] anomaly [...] a wild goat's head [...].

o 6'6'

[BE] ⸢iz⸣-bu SAG.DU DARA₃ <u> IGI UR.MAḪ ⸢GAR⸣ [...]

(o 6') [If] an anomaly is provided with a wild goat's head and lion's eyes [...].

o 7'7'

[BE] ⸢iz⸣-bu ⸢SAG⸣.DU SUN₂ u₂-lu SAG.DU UR.MAḪ ⸢GAR⸣ [...]

(o 7') [If] an anomaly is provided with a wild cow's head or a lion's head [...].

o 8'8'

[BE] iz-bu SAG.DU GU₄! GAR [...]

(o 8') [If] an anomaly is provided with an ox's head [...].

o 9'9'

BE iz-bu ⸢SAG⸣.DU ( o ) ⸢ANŠE⸣ ( o ) GAR ( o ) [...]

(o 9') If an anomaly is provided with a donkey's head [...].

o 10'10'

BE iz-bu ⸢SAG⸣.DU ⸢ANŠE⸣.KUR.RA GAR [...]

(o 10') If an anomaly is provided with a horse's head [...].

o 11'11'

BE iz-bu ⸢SAG⸣.DU ANŠE.KUR.RA u pa-gar UR.MAḪ GAR [...] GI x [...]

(o 11') If an anomaly is provided with a horse's head and a lion's body [...].

o 12'12'

BE iz-bu SAG.DU [...] GAR [...] ⸢EN⸣ E₂ UŠ₂-ma munusAGRIG  ; [...] BIRaḫ-ma x

(o 12') If an anomaly is provided with the head of [...]: [...] the house's owner will die and the housekeeper [...] will be scattered and ....

o 13'13'

BE ⸢iz⸣-bu ⸢SAG.DU⸣ [...] IB? GAR E₂.GAL NUN BIRaḫ [...] x MANe UMUŠ E₂ KI E₂ KUR₂ir UŠ₂-ma  ; ( o ) ⸢ZI?⸣ut ( o ) LUGAL ( o ) IM.GI

(o 13') If an anomaly is provided with the head of [...] ...: the prince's palace will be scattered; [...] ...; changing of decisions; he will change (his) house for (another) house; he will die and attack of the rebel king.

o 14'14'

[... NAM].LU₂.U₁₈.LU u pa-gar UR.GI₇ GAR E₂.GAL NUN BIRaḫ

(o 14') [...] is provided with the [...] of a human being and a dog's body: the prince's palace will be scattered.

o 15'15'

[...] ⸢NAM⸣.LU₂.U₁₈.LU u pa-gar ANŠE GAR DUMU LUGAL AŠ.TE AD-šu₂ DAB

(o 15') [...] is provided with the [...] of a human being and a donkey's body: the king's son will seize his father's throne.

o 16'16'

[...] ⸢NAM⸣.LU₂.U₁₈.LU pa-gar UDU.NITA₂ GAR SU.GU₇ ina KUR GAL₂ši

(o 16') [...] is provided with the [...] of a human being and a ram's body: there will be famine in the land.

o 17'17'

[BE iz-bu] ⸢SAG⸣.DU MUŠ ( 2 ) GAR ( o ) NUN gišTUKUL-MEŠ-šu₂ UGU gišTUKUL KUR₂-šu₂ URU₃-MEŠ

(o 17') [If an anomaly] is provided with a snake's head: as for the prince - his armies will protect (him) against the enemy's army.

o 18'18'

[BE iz-bu] ⸢SAG⸣.DU MUŠ KUN KA₅.A GAR BA₃ut LUGAL—GI.NA ša₂ KUR i-be-lu

(o 18') [If an anomaly] is provided with a snake's head and a fox's tail: omen of Sargon who ruled over the land.

o 19'19'

[BE iz-bu] SAG.DU BAL.GIku₆ ( o ) GAR ( o ) ḪI.GAR ina KUR GAL₂ši

(o 19') [If an anomaly] is provided with a tortoise's head: there will be a rebellion in the land.

o 20'20'

[BE iz]-⸢bu⸣ SAG.DU NIG₂.BUN₂.NAku₆ ŠEG₃ ina ANe LA₂-ma KUR SU.GU₇ IGI

(o 20') [If an anomaly] is provided with a turtle's head: rain from heaven will decrease and the land will experience famine.

o 21'21'

[BE iz]-⸢bu⸣ SAG.DU TI₈mušen ( o ) GAR ( o ) KUR ina ṭe-em NI₂-ša₂-ma i-tak-kal

(o 21') [If an] anomaly is provided with an eagle's head: the land(s) will devastate each other on their own initiative.

o 22'22'

[BE] ⸢iz⸣-bu SAG.DU mušenURU.ḪUL.LA : MUŠEN GAR KUR i-ḫar-ru-[ub]

(o 22') [If] an anomaly is provided with an owl's (or): a bird's head: the land will be deserted.

o 23'23'

[BE] ⸢iz⸣-bu SAG.DU UGAmušen : MUŠEN ( o ) GAR ( o ) NUN ( o ) UGU KUR-šu₂ ⸢GIG⸣ [x]

(o 23') [If] an anomaly is provided with a raven's (or): a bird's head: the prince will cause trouble to his land.

o 24'24'

[BE] ⸢iz⸣-bu SAG.DU KUR.GImušen GAR ŠEG₃ ina KUR LA₂ [...]

(o 24') [If] an anomaly is provided with a goose's head: rain will diminish in the land.

o 25'25'

[BE iz]-bu SAG.DU UDU.NITA₂ u pa-gar UR.MAḪ ( o ) GAR BA₃ut ⸢AN⸣ [x (x)] x x ŠUB x x [...]

(o 25') [If] an anomaly is provided with a ram's head and a lion's body: omen of the ... [...].

o 26'26'

[BE iz]-⸢bu⸣ mut-ta-at SAG.DU-šu₂ ša₂ 15 la₃ GAL₂ [...]

(o 26') [If an] anomaly - the right half of its head does not exist [...].

o 27'27'

[BE iz-bu] ⸢mut⸣-ta-at SAG.DU-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 la₃ GAL₂ [...]

(o 27') [If an anomaly] - the left half of its head does not exist [...].

o 28'28'

[...] x x x [...]

(o 28') [...] ... [...].

rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] x x x [...]

(r 1') [...] ... [...].

r 2'2'

[...] x AL NUN [...]

(r 2') [...] ... the prince [...].

r 3'3'

[... i]-⸢na⸣-aṭ-ṭa-al u U₂ [...]

(r 3') [...] will look and ... [...].

r 4'4'

[...] x x [...] x pur-ru-ur IRI ZA₃-ka KUR₂ [...]

(r 4') [...] scattering of the city; (as for) your border the enemy [...].

r 5'5'

[...]-ma la₃ [... SU].GU₇ ina KUR GAL₂ši-ma UN-MEŠ NIG₂.ŠU-<<šu₂>>-ši-na ana KI ŠUBut x

(r 5') [...] and not [...] there will be famine in the land and (as for) the people - their goods will fall to the ground.

r 6'6'

[...] UGU [...] [iz]-zi-iz UMUŠ KUR MANni

(r 6') [...] over [...] stands: decisions for the land will change.

r 7'7'

[...] ⸢SAGŠU?⸣ [ka]-bi-iš ŠUBti₃ EREN₂ni

(r 7') [...] is wearing a headcloth: fall of the army.

r 8'8'

[...] x x [x (x)] UGU [...] x ŠI E₃!(U₄)a NIGINir ta-as-ḫir-ta-šu₂ i-saḫ-ḫur-šu₂

(r 8') [...] ... [...] over [...] ...emerges (and) is turned: he will seek for him his tashīrtu.

r 9'9'

[...] x x [...] x ⸢ŠID?⸣ KALA TAG-ma x GAZ-šu₂

(r 9') [...] ... [...] ... he will grasp (him) violently and ... will kill him.

r 10'10'

[BE iz]-⸢bu UGU-šu₂ ke-e-er⸣ [(x)] x x x x [...]-šu₂ ina UGU SAG.DU-šu₂ GAR mar-ši-it KUR GAL₂!(UTU)ši

(r 10') [If an] anomaly - (its) forehead is rubbed [...] is provided with its [...] on the top of its head: there will be livestock (for) the country.

r 11'11'

BE iz-bu UGU-šu₂ la₃ [... ni]-ṣir-tu₂ E₂.GAL ana KUR₂ E₃ ana gišTUKUL ŠUBti₃ EREN₂ni

(r 11') If an anomaly - its skull is not [...] the palace's treasure will be given out to the enemy; fall of the army in battle.

r 12'12'

BE iz-bu UGU-šu₂ ke-e-er! <<ER>> [...] ŠUBti₃ EREN₂ni

(r 12') If an anomaly - its skull is rubbed [...] fall of the army.

r 13'13'

BE iz-bu UGU-šu₂ ḫa-bi-iš KUR₂ ina ŠA₃ KUR IŠ-šu₂ x [...] x-⸢ma IRI⸣ ša₂ ZA₃-ka TIqe₂ u₂-lu BAD₃-ka ina-qar

(r 13') If an anomaly - its skull is crushed: the enemy will ... in the heart of the land; [...] and he will take your border town or he will demolish your city wall.

r 14'14'

BE iz-bu UGU-šu₂ DU RI x [...] ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 14') If an anomaly - its skull ... there will be [...] in the land.

r 15'15'

BE iz-bu GU-MEŠ 4 ša₂-a-tu [...] MAN [...]

(r 15') If an anomaly - 4 filaments are knoted together [...] will change [...].

r 16'16'

BE iz-bu UGU-šu₂ ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂[...] x [...]

(r 16') If an anomaly - its skull (gloss: new break) [...] ... [...].

r 17'17'

BE iz-bu UGU-šu₂ ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂[...]

(r 17') If an anomaly - its skull (gloss: new break) [...].

r 18'18'

BE iz-bu gul-gul-lat-su : sa-pi-iḫ : bul-lu-lat [...]

(r 18') If an anomaly - its skull: is scattered (or): is numb [...].

r 19'19'

BE iz-bu nak-kap-ta-šu₂ pe-ta-at : a-pa-at x [...]

(r 19') If an anomaly - its brow is open (or): is veiled ... [...].

r 20'20'

BE iz-bu UZU GIM su-ru-um GURu₂ ina SAG.KI-šu₂ [...]

(r 20') If an anomaly - the flesh on its forehead is twisted like bowels [...].

r 21'21'

BE iz-bu ina SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 GU ša₂ GIM UZU E₃-ma [...]

(r 21') If an anomaly - a filament that is like flesh emerges from its right temple and [...].

r 22'22'

BE iz-bu ina SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 GU ša₂ GIM UZU E₃-⸢ma⸣ [...]

(r 22') If an anomaly - a filament that is like flesh emerges from its left temple and [...].

r 23'23'

[...] GU₂-šu₂ 1-ma LUGAL KI IGI-šu₂ [...]

(r 23') [...] its neck is one: the king, wherever [...] his face [...]

r 24'24'

[...] x ⸢KUR⸣ [...] x x [...]

(r 24') [...] ... the land [...] ... [...].
